rulings.Case Title: RUBEN RODRIGUEZ VS. STONESTOWN GALLERIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf'S
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 5, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANTS GENERAL GROWTH PROPERTIES INC, STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P.'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf's Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted per the stipulation of the parties. The Court will sign the proposed stipulation and order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to brad@kaplans.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY HOULIHAN VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 5, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff cannot demonstrate the existence of a dangerous condition or causation. Plaintiff admits that he never saw any gravel/debris in his lane where the accident occurred. (Dept. of Transportation undisputed material facts 13-14.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHINESE MERCHANTS REALTY ASSOCIATION AKA SEE YIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Evidentiary, Issue And Monetary Sanctions Agaisnt X-Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 5, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT CHINESE MERCHANTS REALTY ASSOCIATION, AKA SEE YIP CHINESE MERCHANTS ASSOCIATION'S, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Evidentiary, Issue And Monetary Sanctions Against X-Deft Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to address the following issues: (1) The reason counsel for Mr. Fong was unable to contact him after the court issued its order on the motion for protective order; (2) the reason Mr. Fong was unavailable to meet with his counsel or appear for his deposition until the last week of February; and (3) whether Mr. Fong's deposition proceeded on February 28 and, if so, what procedures were utilized to minimize the impact of the deposition proceedings on him. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: YIBRAH TESFA VS. ANNE STAUBOLL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner/Plaintiff Yibrah Tesfa'S Order To Show Cause Why Peremptory Writ Should Not Issue (Per Ex Parte Order Of 12/21/12)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 5, 2013, Line 6, Petitioner/Plaintiff Yibrah Tesfa'S Order To Show Cause Why Peremptory Writ Should Not Issue (Per Ex Parte Order Of 12/21/12). Off calendar. Due to Respondent's determination to waive the requirement that a conservator be appointed for Petitioner, the matter is now moot. The Court declines to exercise its discretion to resolve the issues raised in this writ notwithstanding the change in factual circumstances. Petitioner fails to show that the issue is likely to recur as an exception to the mootness doctrine. rulings.Case Title: SORIBA BANGOURA VS. UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Noticeof Motion And Motion To Remove, Dismiss And Ban Uses Such As During The Trial The Declarations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 5, 2013, Line 10, PLAINTIFF SORIBA BANGOURA Motion To Remove, Dismiss And Ban Uses Such As During The Trial The Declarations Denied. Plaintiff has cited no legal basis to remove, dismiss or ban the declarations filed by Defendant on January 16, 2013. This is without prejudice to Plaintiff filing an opposition to Defendant's motion for summary judgment or objections to the declarations in connection with his opposition to Defendant's motion for summary judgment. The request of French to English and English to French interpretation service at the hearing is also denied. The Court has no legal duty to provide a court interpreter in this case, and in any event, Plaintiff has not shown that he is incapable of understanding the English language or is incapable of expressing himself in the English language. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH, A NON-PROFIT CORP VS. SHENG KEE OF CALIFORNIA, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Court Approval And Entry Of Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 5, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH, A NON-PROFIT CORP Motion For Court Approval And Entry Of Consent Judgment Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
